design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore asylum and surreal worlds, solve puzzles, interact with NPCs, uncover fragmented memories through environmental and narrative clues, reach final challenge to advance to next chapter
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Psychological horror derived from reality/unreality ambiguity; player uncertainty about whether events are real or Max's coma-induced hallucinations; guilt and trauma surrounding loss and moral compromise
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Uncovering hidden truth through investigation while experiencing surreal psychological horror; piecing together fragmented identity and memories
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game deliberately blurs reality and imagination, making it unclear which world state is 'real'; embraces psychological discomfort and existential dread as primary design pillars rather than action or challenge; treats horror as thematic and atmospheric rather than mechanical; narrative is introspective (internal guilt/coma-state) rather than external threat-driven

difficulty.lives_system: Non-existent; failure in final challenge paths returns player to start of path without progress loss
difficulty.checkpointing: Implicit checkpoint system: reaching end of challenge path triggers cinematic and auto-advances to next chapter; no traditional game over state exists
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal mechanical difficulty; challenge is primarily cognitive (puzzle-solving, exploration, memory reconstruction) rather than reflexive; game prioritizes narrative accessibility over mechanical punishment

progression.unlock_mechanism: Chapter-based progression; each chapter represents distinct world/atmosphere; completing final challenge of chapter unlocks next chapter; chapters accessed through story momentum rather than player choice
replayability_hooks: Multiple distinct atmospheric worlds encourage replay for environmental storytelling
Narrative twist (coma state reveal) incentivizes replaying to recontextualize events
Character perspective shifts (Max as Sarah, as Grimwall) offer varied narrative viewpoints

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through distinct atmospheric level design (malformed children town, demented circus, alien hive, Aztec village)
Isometric perspective and non-tiled 2D navigation emphasize environmental exploration
Character dialogue and NPC interaction for clue-gathering and exposition
Symbolic world shifts reflecting psychological states (different identities in different worlds)
Ambiguity between 'real world' and 'imaginary world' as narrative tool

faction_indicators: Dr. Jacob Morgan (antagonist) represents institutional compromise and profiteering in medicine
Mercy Foundation represents ethical scientific research vs. profitable but insufficient treatments
Max's internal conflict between professional ambition and moral responsibility
danger_signals: Psychological horror atmosphere signaling existential threat
Malevolent alien 'Mother' entity ruling deformed children
Death of sister Sarah from disease driving protagonist's trauma
Car tampering suggesting external threat to protagonist's cure research
environmental_storytelling: Dilapidated sanitarium setting establishes institutional decay
Surreal world transitions reflect fragmented consciousness
Distinct visual atmospheres (circus, hive, Aztec temple) externalize internal psychological states
Deformed children and monstrous entities represent guilt manifestations
Bandaged protagonist face symbolizes hidden identity and memory loss
